children.text: Access to information with State licensing agencies; election and responsibilities of Committee of Blind Vendors
In addition to other requirements imposed in this title

children.tail: See References in Text note below.
et seq.] upon State licensing agencies, such agencies shall—

children.text: provide to each blind licensee access to all relevant financial data, including quarterly and annual financial reports, on the operation of the State vending facility program;
conduct the biennial election of a Committee of Blind Vendors who shall be fully representative of all blind licensees in the State program,

children.tail: So in original. The comma probably should be a semicolon.

children.text: insure that such committee’s responsibilities include (A) participation, with the State agency, in major administrative decisions and policy and program development, (B) receiving grievances of blind licensees and serving as advocates for such licensees, (C) participation, with the State agency, in the development and administration of a transfer and promotion system for blind licensees, (D) participation, with the State agency, in developing training and retraining programs, and (E) sponsorship, with the assistance of the State agency, of meetings and instructional conferences for blind licensees.
This title, referred to in text, is title II of

children.tail: , known as the “Randolph-Sheppard Act Amendments of 1974”. For complete classification of such title to the Code, see Short Title of 1974 Amendment note set out under

children.text: The Randolph-Sheppard Act, referred to in text, is

children.tail: , also popularly known as the Randolph-Sheppard Vending Stand Act, which is classified generally to this chapter. For complete classification of this Act to the Code, see Short Title note set out under

children.text: Section was enacted as part of the Randolph-Sheppard Act Amendments of 1974, and not as part of the Randolph-Sheppard Act which comprises this chapter.

children.tail: , including provisions of section 209 thereof which enacted this section, were originally contained in H.R. 14225, 93rd Congress, Second Session, which was pocket-vetoed during the 31-day intrasession adjournment of the 93rd Congress for the Congressional elections in November, 1974.

children.text: Pursuant to an order of the United States District Court for the District of Columbia (

children.tail: , D.C.D.C. 1976, 412 F.Supp. 353) H.R. 14225 was deemed to have become law without the approval of the President on
. Therefore, for purposes of codification, this section should be deemed to have been enacted by
, in exactly the same manner as it was enacted by
